title: Anisopetala inflata
taxonomy: Anisopetala inflata is classified as a species within the family Anisophyllaceae. This family is a part of the order Proteales, which is a group of flowering plants. The order Proteales is further classified under the clade Rosidae, a large and diverse group of flowering plants. The Rosidae clade is a subclass within the larger class Magnoliopsida, also known as dicotyledonous plants. This classification is based on the plant's anatomy and evolutionary relationships.
uses: Anisopetala inflata has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its purported health benefits.  The plant is said to be used for its anti-inflammatory properties to treat various ailments.  Anisopetala inflata is also believed to have antioxidant properties that may help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress.  Its use in traditional medicine has also been linked to potential benefits in treating digestive issues and fever.  While its efficacy has not been extensively scientifically proven, Anisopetala inflata continues to be studied for its potential medicinal uses.
